characters.description: Uru's Architect and the leader responsible for the temple-city. Working with Blapy and Fractal, Ruwen chooses the site for a portal district and begins shaping a dungeon partnership while preparing the southern border for war.
characters.name: Ruwen Starfield

characters.description: A powerful ally who advises Ruwen on the new portal district. She proposes an economy linking dungeon access, city taxes, services, and offerings.
characters.name: Blapy

characters.description: Ruwen's dungeon keeper, able to appear through and travel within a crystal network. Fractal joins Ruwen and Blapy in selecting land beside the temple-city for a portal and a broad settlement area for dungeon inhabitants.
characters.name: Fractal

characters.description: Ruwen's healer companion, present with him at the restored temple. She commands a series of prayer spells whose costs grow increasingly severe, giving the group powerful aid that must be used with care.
characters.name: Hamma

characters.description: A cultivator companion of Ruwen and his step-form teacher, present with him and the rest of the group at the restored temple.
characters.name: Sift

characters.description: A restored shade who remains at the temple with Ruwen, Hamma, and Sift as an ally of the group.
characters.name: Shade Companion

characters.description: Ruwen's sentient adviser and memory specialist. She combines copied temple holdings into a searchable personal library, giving him rapid access to information as he rebuilds his class path and plans for war.
characters.name: Soulbound Adviser

characters.description: A sentient intelligence that oversees the restored temple. Its stored duplicate of the city library becomes available to Ruwen through his adviser.
characters.name: Temple Caretaker

characters.description: A returning former Black Pyramid librarian who approaches the restored temple beside the Worker lodge leader.
characters.name: Former Black Pyramid Librarian

characters.description: A returning Worker ally and lodge leader who approaches the restored temple beside the former Black Pyramid librarian.
characters.name: Worker Lodge Leader

characters.description: Sift's turtle companion. Research suggests that her kind may be ancient travelers between dimensions, able to alter their size and carry passengers, but the full extent of that possibility is not yet confirmed.
characters.name: Shelly

characters.description: Ruwen's panther companion, who can assume a large guardian form.
characters.name: Whiskers

characters.description: A diamond-level cultivator leader who listens to Ruwen's proposal for a settlement where cultivators would receive equal rights. His people need safety from hostile forces, while Ruwen needs defenders for the city.
characters.name: Phoenix

characters.description: A topaz-level cultivator leader among those considering Ruwen's offer of equal status and land. Her decision matters to a possible alliance between the displaced cultivators and the vulnerable temple-city.
characters.name: Willow

characters.description: The divine name used by Blapy. Ruwen learns this connection from library records showing that Miranda has kept Penn's legendary books checked out indefinitely.
characters.name: Miranda

characters.description: The author of two legendary books hidden from the Black Pyramid library. Their whereabouts and importance remain unknown when Ruwen discovers their records.
characters.name: Penn

characters.description: A chameleon and former senior fetcher whom Ruwen rehires to help restore the Black Pyramid library.
characters.name: Ipper

characters.description: A spider and former senior librarian whom Ruwen rehires to help restore the Black Pyramid library.
characters.name: Quinn

characters.description: Sift's parents, whom Ruwen must meet to complete the Bamboo Viper Clan binding ritual.
characters.name: Sift's Parents

characters.description: An ascended master known to have found her soul without becoming an apostate.
characters.name: Kai

characters.description: Sisters who established the Bamboo Viper Clan. They test Ruwen's reflexes and understanding of the step forms, accept him as worthy, and establish the conditions for his timed ascent of Mount Sorrow.
characters.name: Mist and Thorn

characters.description: A desperate Mount Sorrow climber who attacks Ruwen while searching for his missing grandson. He offers guidance up the mountain in exchange for Ruwen's help finding the boy.
characters.name: Pine

characters.description: An unnamed child of ten who began Mount Sorrow's trial before he was ready. His grandfather Pine searches for him and enlists Ruwen's aid during the ascent.
characters.name: Pine's Grandson

characters.description: An infernal woman trapped by a snare during the Mount Sorrow trial. Ruwen frees her and transfers some of his limited time to her, receiving a blood-name oath that allows him to summon her through a wrist rune.
characters.name: Echo

characters.description: The first founder of the Bamboo Viper tradition and master of its concealed Shadow Steps. She explains that the hidden discipline attacks spirit pathways and offers Ruwen a path toward it if he can pass a soul test.
characters.name: Dusk

characters.description: The newly revived mother of Ruwen. Alongside Colin, she reunites with Ruwen and admits that they executed the other members of their former expedition.
characters.name: Clarissa Starfield

characters.description: The newly revived father of Ruwen. He and Clarissa reunite with Ruwen and disclose their responsibility for the deaths of their expedition companions.
characters.name: Colin Starfield

characters.description: A newly revived former Deepwell priest who previously revived Ruwen's parents and knows Clarissa Starfield.
characters.name: Revived Deepwell Priest

characters.description: The revived Deepwell priest whom temple records confirm is Hamma's biological father, despite his denial that he has a family.
characters.name: Hamma's Father

characters.description: A group of six resurrected leaders from the city's earlier age, drawn from the order, fighter, worker, mage, observer, and merchant classes. They awaken divided by the conflict that preceded the city's destruction.
characters.name: Revived Elders

characters.description: A volatile celestial remnant placed in Ruwen's care by Blapy. She requires Ruwen to take Xavier on dangerous missions, making him both a new responsibility and a potentially formidable addition to the group.
characters.name: Xavier

characters.description: A Naktos tunnel mage who ambushes Ruwen with paired portal bands and explosive excavation rods. Ruwen survives the attack, severs one of her arms, and takes a portal band before escaping.
characters.name: Stone Carver

characters.description: Five high-level Naktos specialists who operate as a coordinated delaying force: a priest, a mage, a shield fighter, a dual-blade fighter, and a dagger-wielding observer. They block Ruwen's party during the field assault.
characters.name: Retribution Squad

characters.description: A deity whose domain includes the restored temple-city. Ruwen begins the book as Uru's Architect, charged with securing the southern border against Naktos.
characters.name: Uru

characters.description: A hostile deity whose campaign threatens Uru's lands. His forces and agents are already the central military danger as Ruwen begins organizing the restored temple-city and its allies for the coming invasion.
characters.name: Naktos

characters.description: A covert ally who reports the Legion's plan to execute the shade companion's brother beyond revival range. The infiltrator can escape the group's notice and coordinates intelligence for the rescue mission.
characters.name: Infiltrator Ally

characters.description: The shade companion's brother, whom the Legion plans to execute beyond revival range. Ruwen's group adopts a step-championship cover for a mission to rescue him.
characters.name: Shade Companion's Brother
